title: Merge two sorted arrays without extra space

content.problemStatement:
Given two sorted integer arrays nums1 and nums2, merge both the arrays into a single array sorted in non-decreasing order.


The final sorted array should be stored inside the array nums1 and it should be done in-place.

 Array nums1 has a length of m + n, where the first m elements denote the elements of nums1 and rest are 0s whereas nums2 has a length of n.
content.inputFormat: Two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, and two integers m and n representing the number of valid elements in nums1 and nums2 respectively.
content.outputFormat: The modified array nums1 with the elements of nums2 merged into it in sorted order.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Two pointers technique to merge two sorted arrays into one in-place.

code.Python3:
class Solution:
    def merge(self, nums1, m, nums2, n):
        i = m - 1
        j = n - 1
        k = m + n - 1
        while i >= 0 and j >= 0:
            if nums1[i] > nums2[j]:
                nums1[k] = nums1[i]
                i -= 1
            else:
                nums1[k] = nums2[j]
                j -= 1
            k -= 1
        while j >= 0:
            nums1[k] = nums2[j]
            k -= 1
            j -= 1
# Example usage
nums1 = [1, 3, 5, 0, 0, 0]
nums2 = [2, 4, 6]
m, n = 3, 3
Solution().merge(nums1, m, nums2, n)
print(nums1)
